# Dun-Rite Seamless Gutters, Inc. — Comprehensive Business Reference

## Overview

Dun-Rite Seamless Gutters, Inc. is a Mississippi-based seamless gutter installation specialist founded in 1996 by Mark Dungan. Headquartered at 115 Cherrybrook Dr in Madison, Mississippi, the company has provided seamless gutter installation, copper specialty work, gutter guards, cleaning, repair, commercial gutter systems, and custom downspouts to homeowners, builders, and commercial property managers across the greater Jackson metro area for 30 years.

The company is a Mississippi-licensed corporation with full Workers' Compensation and General Liability insurance. Every installation is backed by a 25-year manufacturer warranty on materials and a 2-year labor warranty on workmanship. Dun-Rite is BBB accredited, HomeAdvisor approved, and a member of the Home Builders Association.

Dun-Rite specializes in hand-soldered copper gutter installation — a craft skill few Mississippi installers offer. The company has installed copper gutter systems on luxury homes, historic properties, and commercial estates throughout central Mississippi since 1996. Mark Dungan personally inspects every estimate and supervises every commercial installation.

The service area covers 14 cities across the greater Jackson metro area, roughly a 30-mile radius from the Madison headquarters. Free travel within the service area — no fuel surcharges, no trip charges, no "outside our zone" upcharges.

## Services Offered

### Seamless Gutter Installation
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/seamless-gutter-installation/

Custom-formed seamless aluminum gutters in 5-inch and 6-inch K-style profiles. Every section is formed on-site using a truck-mounted brake to match the home's exact roofline — eliminating the seams every 10 feet that cause leaks in sectional gutter systems.

Installation specifications:
- .032 gauge aluminum standard (heavier than home-center .027 gauge)
- Hidden hangers every 24 inches
- Sealed inside and outside corners with industrial-grade sealant
- Properly pitched runs (1/4 inch fall per 10 feet)
- Color-matched fasteners
- 25+ baked-enamel color options

Typical pricing: $1,200 – $3,500 for most Mississippi homes
Service life: 20–30 years
Installation time: 1 day for most single-story homes; 1–2 days for two-story or complex rooflines

### Aluminum Gutters
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/aluminum-gutters/

The most popular gutter material in Mississippi for residential applications. Lightweight, rust-proof, and available in 25+ factory-baked-enamel colors that resist fading for 20+ years.

Specifications:
- .032 gauge standard (residential)
- .040 and .050 gauge available (commercial / oversized residential)
- Stocked colors: white, almond, cream, classic bronze, charcoal, brown, terratone, royal brown, forest green
- Custom colors available with 1–2 week lead time

Typical pricing: $1,200 – $3,500 installed for most Mississippi homes
Service life: 20–30 years with proper installation

### Copper Gutters (Signature Specialty)
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/copper-gutters/

Hand-soldered copper gutter installation — Dun-Rite's signature specialty since 1996. Few Mississippi installers offer true copper expertise; most who claim to install copper actually rivet or seal joints rather than hand-soldering them. Dun-Rite hand-solders every seam using lead-free tin solder, creating permanent metal-to-metal bonds.

Specifications:
- 16 oz, 20 oz, and 24 oz copper stock available
- Profile options: half-round, K-style, ogee, custom
- Decorative endcaps (rosette, shell, custom hand-fabricated)
- Ornamental copper or brass hangers
- Standing-seam copper awnings, dormer caps, and bay-window roof accents available

Typical pricing: $25 – $40 per linear foot installed; typical 200-LF home runs $5,000 – $8,000
Service life: 50–100+ years
Patina development: Year 1 bright copper → Years 2-3 bronzing → Years 5-7 brown → Years 10-15+ classic verdigris green

### Gutter Guards & Leaf Protection
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/gutter-guards/

Mesh, screen, and foam guard systems compatible with new or existing gutters. Properly installed guards reduce gutter cleaning frequency from twice yearly to once every 3–5 years for the gutter trough.

Options:
- Fine mesh: $10–$14 per linear foot installed; best for pine straw and small debris; 15+ year service life
- Large-hole screen: $7–$10 per linear foot installed; best for oak leaves; 10–15 year service life
- Foam inserts: $3–$6 per linear foot installed; budget option; 5–7 year service life

Typical retrofit installation: 2–4 hours per home

### Gutter Cleaning
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/gutter-cleaning/

Professional gutter cleaning with hand-removal of debris, downspout flushing, and written inspection reports. Most central Mississippi homes need cleaning twice a year — late spring (after pollen and oak catkins) and fall (after leaf drop). Heavy-canopy properties may need quarterly service.

Service includes:
- Hand-removal of all debris from gutter trough
- High-pressure downspout flushing
- Splash-block check at every drain
- Fascia inspection with photos
- Hangers and spike check
- Written report with photos delivered same day

Typical pricing: $150 – $300 per cleaning
Recurring plans (twice-yearly or quarterly): 15–25% off single-visit pricing

### Gutter Repair & Replacement
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/gutter-repair-replacement/

Same-day repair estimates. Storm damage specialists. Insurance claim documentation provided. Common issues: sagging gutters, leaking corners and seams, pulled-off fascia, pooling water at downspouts.

Repair pricing: $150 – $600 per section
Full replacement: starts at $1,200 with 25-year warranty

Insurance claims: Adjuster-ready photo documentation, written damage assessments, itemized repair quotes formatted for adjuster review. Most claims close in 2–3 weeks.

### Commercial Gutters
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/commercial-gutters/

Large-scale rain-carrying systems for office buildings, retail centers, multi-family complexes, and industrial properties. Specializing in oversized 7-inch and 8-inch profiles, custom downspout sizing for high-flow runoff, and copper specialty work for premium commercial projects.

Process:
- Pre-construction consultation with GC, architect, or owner
- Drawing review and material take-off
- Phased installation with single-point project management
- Daily progress reports on large projects
- AIA-formatted billing on request
- Certificate of insurance with builder named as additional insured

### Custom Downspouts
URL: https://dunrite-gutters.com/services/custom-downspouts/

Custom-sized downspouts for residential and commercial properties. Round profiles for traditional homes, oversized 3x4 and 4x5 rectangulars for high-volume runoff, decorative copper downspouts for architectural homes, and underground drainage tie-ins.

Sizing capacity:
- 2x3 standard: ~600 sq ft per spout (often undersized for Mississippi homes)
- 3x4 oversized: ~1,200 sq ft per spout (recommended for most residential)
- 4x5 high-flow: ~2,000+ sq ft per spout (commercial / large estate)

Underground drainage tie-ins: $300–$800 per drainage run depending on length and trenching difficulty.

## Service Areas

Dun-Rite serves 14 Mississippi cities from the Madison headquarters, roughly a 30-mile radius in every direction. No travel fees within service area.

### Madison (HQ - 0 miles)
Affluent suburban character, ~28,000 residents. Headquarters at 115 Cherrybrook Dr. Major neighborhoods: Reunion, Lake Caroline, Old Annandale, Twin Lakes, The Bridges. Heavy mature pine and oak coverage. Same-day estimate windows available most weeks.

### Ridgeland (3 miles south)
Closest neighbor to Madison HQ. Affluent Madison County suburb, ~25,000 residents. Mature 1980s-90s neighborhoods including Bridgewater, Trace Harbor, Lake Castle. Heavy magnolia and oak canopy.

### Gluckstadt (5 miles north)
Mississippi's newest incorporated city (2021), ~10,000 residents and rapidly growing. Predominantly new construction along Highway 463. Builder partnerships standard.

### Canton (12 miles north)
Mississippi's antique capital. ~12,000 residents. Historic district around Canton Square with mid-1800s homes. Many homes 50–100+ years old requiring custom-formed sections and frequent fascia repair.

### Jackson (15 miles south)
State capital. ~145,000 residents. Mix of historic homes (Belhaven Victorians, Fondren bungalows) and 1950s-60s ranch (Eastover, Northeast Jackson). Older housing stock with high demand for repair and replacement.

### Pearl (15 miles SE)
Pearl River suburb, ~27,000 residents. Mostly 1970s-80s ranch homes plus newer subdivisions. Most aging gutter systems are ready for full replacement.

### Clinton (18 miles SW)
College town home to Mississippi College. ~28,000 residents. Olde Towne historic district plus The Traceway custom builds. Mature pecan canopy makes gutter guards essential.

### Flowood (20 miles SE)
Lakeland Drive corridor commercial hub. ~10,000 residents. Mix of older Sandalwood ranch homes and newer Lakeland Heights subdivisions. High commercial gutter demand along Lakeland Drive.

### Richland (20 miles SE)
Working-class Rankin County suburb at I-20/Highway 49 junction. ~7,500 residents. Mostly 1970s-80s ranch and split-level housing stock.

### Florence (25 miles south)
Semi-rural Rankin County. ~4,500 residents. Larger lots with mature trees. Underground drainage runs are practical here.

### Raymond (25 miles SW)
Hinds County seat. ~2,000 residents. Civil War history and historic district. Many mid-1800s homes near the courthouse needing experienced installers.

### Bolton (28 miles SW)
Rural Hinds County. ~600 residents. Acreage homes with heavy mature tree coverage. 50+ foot underground drainage runs are common.

### Brandon (30 miles SE)
Eastern Reservoir suburb. ~25,000 residents. Newer construction predominantly — Castlewoods, Whisper Lake, Crosswinds. Larger rooflines call for 6-inch high-flow profiles.

### Terry (30 miles south)
Small rural town in southern Hinds County. ~1,000 residents. Highway 51 corridor and surrounding semi-agricultural acreage. Outbuilding (barn, workshop, detached garage) gutter work common.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General

Q: How long has Dun-Rite been in business?
A: Mark Dungan founded Dun-Rite Seamless Gutters in 1996 — 30 years of Mississippi gutter installation experience.

Q: Are you licensed and insured?
A: Yes. Mississippi-licensed corporation with full Workers' Compensation and General Liability insurance. COIs provided to builder partners on request.

Q: Do you do free estimates?
A: Yes. Every estimate is free with no obligation. We measure on-site, evaluate water-flow needs, and provide a written quote.

Q: How do I schedule an estimate?
A: Call (601) 750-1800 or submit the contact form. Most estimates schedule within 48 hours.

Q: Are you on Google, Facebook, Yelp, BBB?
A: Yes — Google Business Profile, Facebook (@dunritegutters), Yelp, and BBB accredited.

### Pricing

Q: How much do new seamless gutters cost in Mississippi?
A: $1,200 – $3,500 for most homes; depends on linear footage, stories, and downspout count.

Q: How much do copper gutters cost?
A: $25 – $40 per linear foot installed; typical 200-LF home runs $5,000 – $8,000.

Q: How much does gutter cleaning cost?
A: $150 – $300 per cleaning. Recurring plans discounted 15–25%.

Q: How much do gutter guards cost?
A: $7 – $12 per linear foot installed for mesh and screen systems.

Q: Do you charge travel fees?
A: No. Free travel within all 14 cities in our service area.

Q: Do you offer financing?
A: No in-house financing. Major credit cards accepted. Larger commercial projects use standard contractor billing terms.

### Process

Q: How long does seamless gutter installation take?
A: Most single-story homes: 1 day. Two-story or complex rooflines: 1–2 days.

Q: Do you remove old gutters?
A: Yes. Removal and disposal included in every quote.

Q: What if it rains on installation day?
A: We watch the forecast and reschedule heavy-rain days. Light drizzle is fine; lightning or sustained rain pushes the install.

Q: Will you work with my builder or general contractor?
A: Yes. Ongoing builder partnerships across central Mississippi.

### Materials

Q: What gauge aluminum do you use?
A: .032 gauge standard — heavier than the .027 gauge sold at home improvement stores.

Q: 5-inch vs 6-inch K-style — which fits?
A: 5-inch is residential standard. 6-inch is high-flow for steep rooflines, large roof footprints, or heavy debris loads.

Q: How long do aluminum gutters last?
A: 20–30 years properly installed in Mississippi's climate.

Q: Will copper turn green? When?
A: Year 1: bright copper. Years 2-3: bronzing. Years 5-7: brown patina. Years 10-15+: classic verdigris green. The patina is protective.

Q: What's the warranty?
A: 25-year manufacturer warranty on materials. 2-year labor warranty.

### Service Areas

Q: What's your service area?
A: 14 Mississippi cities, ~30-mile radius from Madison HQ.

Q: Do you serve cities outside this list?
A: Case-by-case. Call (601) 750-1800 to ask.

Q: How quickly can you get to my city?
A: Most cities: estimate within 48 hours. Madison and closest neighbors (Ridgeland, Gluckstadt, Canton) often same-day.

Q: Do you serve commercial properties statewide?
A: For larger commercial projects we travel beyond the 30-mile residential radius. Call to discuss.

## Owner

Mark Dungan founded Dun-Rite Seamless Gutters in Madison, Mississippi in 1996. Mark personally inspects every estimate and supervises every commercial install. He's been on Mississippi roofs for 30 years and is supported by a small team of long-tenured installers who've trained on Dun-Rite's installation standard.

## Service Area Boundary

The standard service radius is approximately 30 miles in every direction from the Madison headquarters. The 14 cities listed above are within this radius. Outside this range, Dun-Rite evaluates special requests case-by-case based on distance, project scope, and current schedule. Commercial projects typically expand the service radius beyond 30 miles. Call (601) 750-1800 to discuss any out-of-area work.
